title: Context Engineering and Repository Grounding · Contract Clinic: Context Refresh
summary: Ground an agent in a large unfamiliar repository without flooding its context. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt context refresh increment in a repository context map and retrieval plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Refresh drift-prone facts and summarize stable discoveries before context compaction.
Detect conflicting instructions and evidence and apply a documented precedence rule.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Predict Context Refresh
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt context engineering. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt context engineering. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Context Refresh contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for refresh drift-prone facts and summarize stable discoveries before context compaction.

content.text: Refresh drift-prone facts and summarize stable discoveries before context compaction. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: State the current model, seek disconfirming evidence, and record what would make the conclusion change.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt context engineering. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Context Refresh, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a repository context map and retrieval plan.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Once loaded, external documentation never needs revalidation. This fails the Context Refresh contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Context Refresh, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional.

steps.title: Build Context Refresh evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-context-refresh-1ysjww" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Context Refresh into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-context-refresh-1ysjww
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Defend the Context Refresh model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Context Refresh applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Refresh drift-prone facts and summarize stable discoveries before context compaction.
